mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by 1.5%+ over 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 2000k signals rising consumer confidence and durable goods demand that drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 2000k growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and consumer prices indicates strong demand pressures that push equity valuations higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening cycle characterized by rising Fed funds rates and declining real sales suggests monetary policy is offsetting economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tight labor markets that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE remains below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production combined with rising inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% in 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns when both INDPRO declines by 1.5%+ and CPIAUCSL rises by 0.5%+ over 2 months.
